justification: Neither output extracts projects or impact highlights, so project_classification and impact_quality are neutral for both. On skill_accuracy, A's additions (Object Detection, CNN, Real-time Inference, Data Preprocessing, Model Training) are genuinely demonstrated in the projects, whereas B adds 'Machine Learning' (a generic term not explicitly used), 'Data Structures', and 'Algorithms' which are coursework names rather than demonstrated skills. A's extra skills are more defensible from project evidence. Both are slightly over-extracted (24 vs 22 skills), so conservatism is similarly penalized. A edges out B on skill_accuracy by grounding additions in actual project work rather than course titles.
